reviews.review_description:
The artwork and museum are rendered beautifully.

Unfortunately for some inexplicable reason the developers chose to add a creepy plastic looking woman that incessantly jabbers away while you're trying to enjoy the paintings and you cannot switch her off.

I tried turning the sound all the way down to get some peace but that only made things worse.

It turns what should be a peaceful visit to a museum into a cheesy horror game. The chatty plastic woman becomes a creepy silent stalker who slowly chases you around the museum. 
Try running away from her and when you turn around there she is, slowly coming  after you.

It's a shame. This could be a fantastic experience I'd gladly pay for but it's been ruined by a very inappropriate, cheesy, and surprisingly creepy feature that cannot be disabled. 

I'd recommend not downloading this one as beautiful as it is because it'll end up being frustratingly disappointing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for pointing this out. We'll release an update soon that allows the guide to be disabled.

reviews.review_description:
This is a very nice experience. The reason for having to drop a star is as follows: 

1 - The guide is helpful with the information but the ai voice is distracting. The physics of the guide is a little janky too. 

2 - Locomotion is a bit rough too. 

The good news is that these should be relatively straightforward to fix. 

Congrats to the developers overall.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! You can disable the guide at any time by pressing the right trigger.

reviews.review_description:
I like going to art museums. This is great. All the best and famous works in one place. 

I can see how some people feel the tour guide is creepy. At least you can have her disappear with the press of a button. Another option is to have her keep her keep her distance unless you summon her. Or have a couple other patrons admiring the pieces so you’re not alone with her. Also her AI voice could be better. 

That being said I look forward to more updates.  Thank you for this.
For the love of god, please get rid of the chittering, stalking sexdoll! Other than that, 5 stars.
It would be nice if you add more galleries, as free or paid DLC.

Please… just do what art quest does but just do it yalls way and take my money… this would be a million times better than art quest. Just do everything they are doing but just do it your way and take my money….

reviews.review_title: Great looking art but no comfort options
reviews.review_description:
The art pieces are really looking great! The frames could look a bit more realistic though. And the font size of the explaining texts on the signs should be bigger, it's hard to read unless you are really close. But my biggest issue is the high walking speed, even when you try to push the joystick just minimally. I am getting sick very quickly. Please add options for movement speed, vignettes during movement and preferably also teleporting! 
This app would be cool for demoing people VR but not when they get motion sickness.
this is a really amazing start and i can't believe that it's a free experience
Beautiful gallery. Put an option to get rid of the tour guide as she sneaks up on you and gives you anxiety

I really enjoyed this app and hope they add more works of art.  You tour a museum and there is a personal tour guide that follows you and tells interesting facts about each exhibit.  The most famous paintings in the world are shown in the tour.  The graphics for each painting looks like it's 3D scanned and very realistic.  This is exactly what I'm looking for when I want a VR museum experience.  Hope it catches on and they update it regularly.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're thrilled you enjoyed the experience. We've put a lot of care into making sure each painting and its frame are as faithful and high-definition as possible. We're already working on new feature and content expansions, stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Felt like I was there.  Loved all the art.   Would happily pay for future paintings.  

Seeing these works is not only inspiring but very  therapeutic.  


Thanks for this!

Bob
